api/client/python/openmeter/operations.purpose: Public re-export facade for all generated Python SDK operation classes (API call builders). Mirrors the same facade pattern as openmeter/models but for _generated/operations — no operation logic is hand-written here.

patterns.name: Wildcard re-export from generated operations
patterns.description: Re-export _operations via wildcard import from _generated.operations. Never implement API call logic in this package.

patterns.name: Optional _patch overlay
patterns.description: Try-import _generated.operations._patch and merge its __all__. Patch symbols extend the generated set non-destructively.
patterns.example:
try: from .._generated.operations._patch import __all__ as _patch_all
except ImportError: _patch_all = []

patterns.name: TYPE_CHECKING guard for patch stubs
patterns.description: Import _patch under `if TYPE_CHECKING:` for IDE support without runtime cost.

patterns.name: Call _patch_sdk() at module load
patterns.description: Always call _patch_sdk() after all re-exports to apply operation-level monkey-patches.

patterns.name: __all__ propagation
patterns.description: Derive __all__ from the generated operations module and extend with patch-only symbols.
patterns.example: __all__ = __all__; __all__.extend([p for p in _patch_all if p not in __all__])

key_file_guides.role: Sole file; thin facade merging generated operation classes + patch overrides into one importable namespace (openmeter.operations.*).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not implement request/response logic here — operations are generated. Any code added here is overwritten on the next make gen-api. Do not import deeper than _operations/_patch from _generated internals.

anti_patterns: Implementing operation or request logic directly in this package — belongs in _generated/operations
Removing the _patch_sdk() call — patch hooks silently will not apply
Manually curating __all__ instead of delegating to the generated module
Adding hand-written operation classes that duplicate generated ones and drift on codegen

decisions.decision: Stable public import path via facade over generated internals
decisions.rationale: Generated operation classes live under _generated and may be reorganised by the generator; the facade decouples consumer imports from generator layout, providing a stable openmeter.operations.* surface.

code_examples.scenario: Full __init__.py structure — the only valid pattern for this package
code_examples.code:
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from .._generated.operations._patch import *

from .._generated.operations._operations import *  # noqa: F401, F403

try:
    from .._generated.operations._patch import __all__ as _patch_all
    from .._generated.operations._patch import *  # noqa: F401, F403
except ImportError:
    _patch_all = []
from .._generated.operations._patch import patch_sdk as _patch_sdk

from .._generated.operations import __all__
__all__ = __all__
__all__.extend([p for p in _patch_all if p not in __all__])
_patch_sdk()

api/client/python/scripts.purpose: Contains the single release script (release.sh) that computes the next PEP-440 pre-release version by querying live PyPI state, stamps it into pyproject.toml and openmeter/_version.py/_commit.py, then invokes `poetry publish --build`. Primary constraint: only 'alpha' is a valid PY_SDK_RELEASE_TAG value; any other tag hard-errors.

patterns.name: PEP-440 alpha versioning via live PyPI query
patterns.description: When PY_SDK_RELEASE_VERSION is absent, the script queries PyPI for the latest alpha release, extracts the pre-release number, increments it, and constructs the next version (e.g. 1.0.0a3). Any new release automation must follow this monotonic increment pattern.
patterns.example: LATEST_VERSION=$(curl -s https://pypi.org/pypi/openmeter/json | jq -r '.releases | keys[] | select(test("a[0-9]+"))' | sort -V | tail -1)

patterns.name: Version normalisation before poetry invocation
patterns.description: Before calling `poetry version`, strip a leading 'v' and convert semver pre-release suffixes (-alpha, -beta) to PEP-440 equivalents (a, b) via sed. Any injected PY_SDK_RELEASE_VERSION must survive this sed pipeline.

patterns.name: Unconditional dist clean before publish
patterns.description: `rm -rf dist` runs before `poetry publish --build` to prevent poetry's interactive prompt about overwriting existing artifacts. Any pre-publish build step must not recreate dist before this clean.

patterns.name: Version/commit stamped into package at build time
patterns.description: The script writes openmeter/_version.py and openmeter/_commit.py as transient side-effects before the build. These must not be committed. The `|| true` silently ignores write failures if openmeter/ is missing.

key_file_guides.role: Single-entry-point release script invoked by CI (sdk-python-dev-release.yaml) with PY_SDK_RELEASE_TAG=alpha or a pre-set PY_SDK_RELEASE_VERSION. Computes version, stamps files, runs `poetry publish --build`.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The `|| true` on printf lines silently ignores write failures for _version.py/_commit.py. Only 'alpha' is accepted as PY_SDK_RELEASE_TAG; any other value exits with error. COMMIT_SHORT_SHA falls back to `git rev-parse --short=12 HEAD`.

anti_patterns: Committing openmeter/_version.py or openmeter/_commit.py — they are written transiently by release.sh and must not be in source control
Setting PY_SDK_RELEASE_TAG to anything other than 'alpha' — the script hard-errors on unknown tags
Calling `poetry build` separately before this script — leftover dist artifacts trigger an interactive poetry prompt that hangs CI
Injecting a PY_SDK_RELEASE_VERSION with unusual formats (e.g. 'v1.0.0-alpha.1') without verifying the sed pipeline produces a valid PEP-440 string
Adding a pre-publish build step that recreates the dist/ directory after the `rm -rf dist` clean

decisions.decision: Version computed from live PyPI state rather than git tags
decisions.rationale: Alpha releases are continuous and not tied to formal git tags; querying PyPI ensures the next published version is always monotonically incremented even if the CI job is re-run.

decisions.decision: poetry used for both build and publish in a single command
decisions.rationale: Keeps the release pipeline to one tool invocation, avoiding a separate `poetry build` step that would require managing dist artifact cleanup independently.

api/client/web.purpose: Tombstone folder documenting that the Web SDK has been superseded by the fetch-based JavaScript SDK at api/client/javascript. Contains only a README redirect; no active code lives here.

key_file_guides.role: Single-file redirect pointing developers to api/client/javascript.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add new files here. Any browser/web SDK work belongs in api/client/javascript.

anti_patterns: Adding new SDK code to this folder — it is deprecated in favour of api/client/javascript
